mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS sits near the zero lower bound, discount rates applied to equity cash flows remain structurally compressed, biasing S&P 500 forward returns positive over a one-month horizon as investors rotate from low-yielding cash into equities.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and forward 20-day returns falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX exhibits negative average forward returns during sustained near-zero rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: A meaningful decline in UNRATE from an elevated post-shock peak signals labor market healing that leads corporate earnings recovery, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-quarter horizon once the decline exceeds a material threshold.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE declines more than 0.5pp over the trailing 3 months from a level above 7%
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 8.1% consistent with elevated post-shock level
failure_condition: In triggered instances, SPX 60-day forward returns are negative or not statistically distinguishable from unconditional baseline (t-test p > 0.05) across at least two independent recovery episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strengthening of retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) captures a durable-and-nondurable demand rebound that jointly signals broad consumer confidence recovery, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon beyond what either series predicts alone.
regime_hint: any regime with synchronized consumer and housing demand recovery
failure_condition: The interaction term (RSAFS momentum x HOUST momentum) shows no incremental predictive power over the univariate RSAFS or HOUST signals, measured by Rank IC improvement below 0.02 over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) reflects expanding manufacturing capacity utilization, a leading indicator of corporate revenue growth that translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over a short one-week horizon as markets price in near-term earnings momentum.
failure_condition: 5-day forward return correlation with INDPRO month-over-month change remains below 0.02 Rank IC over any rolling 1-year window.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery regime, the joint occurrence of low CPIAUCSL inflation and near-zero FEDFUNDS signals that policymakers have room to remain accommodative without inflation risk, extending the equity risk-on window and supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-quarter horizon; outside this regime the same combination signals stagnation risk and the effect reverses.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.14 near zero lower bound
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with accommodative monetary policy and low inflation
failure_condition: During triggered low-rate/low-inflation windows outside acknowledged recession recovery periods, SPX 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of instances, contradicting the regime-conditional positive claim.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp acceleration in nonfarm payroll growth (PAYEMS) signals a rapid labor market rebound that leads consumer spending and earnings upgrades, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon once payroll gains cross a strong threshold.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month change exceeds 500,000 for two consecutive months
regime_hint: early-cycle labor market rebound after sharp job losses
failure_condition: Triggered instances of large payroll gains are followed by negative or flat 20-day SPX returns in more than 50% of cases across at least two recovery episodes.

mechanism_narrative: In an early-cycle recovery regime following a recession trough, rising housing starts (HOUST) signal renewed long-cycle capital commitment by households, foreshadowing durable economic expansion and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-quarter horizon; in late-cycle regimes the same rise signals overheating and predicts weaker returns.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 654k units observed near cyclical recovery lows
failure_condition: During identified early-cycle regimes, HOUST increases are not accompanied by statistically significant positive 60-day SPX forward returns (t-test p > 0.05) relative to baseline.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of a declining UNRATE and rising PAYEMS confirms genuine labor market recovery rather than a statistical artifact of labor force exit, and this dual confirmation drives a short-horizon positive S&P 500 reaction as markets reduce recession risk pricing within one trading week.
regime_hint: any regime with concurrent labor market data releases
failure_condition: The joint UNRATE-decline-and-PAYEMS-increase signal shows no improvement in 5-day forward return predictive accuracy over either single-series signal, with Rank IC gain below 0.02 across a 2-year rolling window.
